title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Failure Case: Constant Tests
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates constant tests increment in a domain constant and configuration policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test boundary values and downstream behavior instead of only constant equality.
Separate units, deploy-time configuration, feature flags, and code constants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Constant Tests evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-constant-tests-txk7z1: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Constant Tests into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-constant-tests-txk7z1:

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Defend the Constant Tests model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Constant Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
